# Architecture Decomposition & Contracts Session — 2026-07-15

**Generated by:** Claude Code (Fable 5), session on branch `claude/architecture-decomposition-review-squ1fy`
**Generated on:** 2026-07-15
**Repository HEAD:** `6f39903` (`governance(register): render regeneration + header backfill`), clean tree
**Repository:** `Crystalka228/Dual-Frontier`
**Input artifact:** [`DUAL_FRONTIER_ARCHITECTURE_DECOMPOSITION_REVIEW_20260715.md`](./DUAL_FRONTIER_ARCHITECTURE_DECOMPOSITION_REVIEW_20260715.md) — external agent review, verified against the same commit `6f39903`; committed alongside this report as the claims source
**Discipline:** read-only over existing docs and code. Deliverables are additive only: this report, the committed input artifact, and seven AUTHORED draft model documents (§7). No existing document or source file was modified.
**Format precedents:** `docs/reports/ARCHITECTURE_RECON_REPORT.md` (fact-first numbered sections, single synthesis at end), `docs/reports/DOCUMENTATION_DUAL_LOAD_DRIFT_REPORT.md` (multi-agent read-only execution model). Quality bar: METHODOLOGY Lessons #7 (transcribe, don't paraphrase) and #8 (prove intermediate states) — every verdict below carries a verbatim doc quote with section anchor and/or a code anchor as `file:line`.

> **Read me first.** Five facts dominate everything below:
>
> 1. **The external review is substantially correct.** Of its 12 claimed Tier-1 contradictions, 10 are CONFIRMED against doc text and code, 1 is REFUTED as stated (C9 — the review mischaracterized `ARCHITECTURE.md`), and 1 is CONFIRMED but reclassified (C5 — the stale side is a Draft document that is *not* on the authority surface). All 9 missing cross-cutting contracts (A0–A8) are confirmed missing or materially incomplete.
> 2. **The authority problem is one level deeper than the review saw.** The canonical text of the two most load-bearing invariants — К-L12 (native scheduling sovereignty) and К-L15 (native bus authority) — lives in `K_CLOSURE_REPORT.md`, a Tier-1 **AUTHORED** document that fails the FRAMEWORK §14.7 authority predicate, yet self-designates as "load-bearing authority surface" (`K_CLOSURE_REPORT.md:76`). The governance predicate itself has no inter-document precedence rule, so the conflicts this report catalogs are formally unarbitrable inside the current framework (§6.1).
> 3. **Two disjoint analyzer programs coexist without acknowledging each other.** The Draft DF_TS family (`ARCHITECTURE_TYPE_SYSTEM.md` + `TRACK_B_ACTIVATION`) legislates Error-only severities, a suppression ban, and anchors on a runtime guard **deleted 2026-05-14**; the shipped DFK/DFL/DF999 regime (`ANALYZER_RULES.md`, 17 real rules, Warning tiers, 2 sanctioned waivers) violates every one of those laws. Neither cites the other (§6.1, N-13).
> 4. **The enforcement layer promised by Tier-1 docs is partially fictional.** `PERFORMANCE.md` (LOCKED) pins budgets to a CI gate — `PerformanceGate` / `tests/DualFrontier.Core.Benchmarks/PerformanceGates.cs` — that does not exist anywhere in the repository; there is **no CI of any kind** in-repo (no `.github/`, no pipelines) (§6.4).
> 5. **Shutdown is the largest un-owned transition.** `NativeWorld` is never deterministically disposed (it is not carried by `GameContext`), `ModIntegrationPipeline.UnloadAll` has zero callers, no native scheduler/bus teardown exists, and a sim thread abandoned after `Join(2000)` timeout can race world finalization (§6.3, N-19). This grounds the review's A2/A3 proposals with concrete evidence it did not have.

---

## 1. Scope & method

**Task.** Verify every claim of the external review against the Tier 1–2 Category A document corpus and the code; independently hunt for gaps that both the owner and the review missed; produce this report; author drafts of the missing cross-cutting models (lifecycle AUTHORED, enrolled in the register).

**Corpus.** All 32 active Category A Tier 1–2 documents (register v2.25: 28 active Tier 1 — 25 in `docs/architecture/`, 3 in `docs/governance/` — plus 4 Tier 2; 2 SUPERSEDED historical excluded), ≈1.49 M characters.

**Execution model.** 8 parallel read-only reader agents (R1–R8) over subsystem-clustered batches, each filling a fixed extraction template per document: ROLE (CURRENT/TARGET/EVIDENCE/MIXED), AUTHORITY claims against a fixed 12-domain vocabulary, CONTRACTS tagged [A0..A8], XREF relations, checkable CODE-CLAIMS, SELF-CONTRADICTIONS, ADMITTED-GAPS, assigned REVIEW-CHECK items, NOVEL observations, and an `AUTHORITATIVE per §14.7` header line. The two largest evidence documents (≈610 K chars) were processed grep-then-read (R8). In parallel, 3 verifier agents (V1–V3) ran 15 code probes (project/fixture inventory; enforcement and staleness sweeps; runtime tracing of bridge, config, internals-consumption, bus semantics, shutdown ordering). Verdicts were joined in the main session context.

**Verdict categories.** CONFIRMED / PARTIALLY / REFUTED / STALE-ALREADY-FIXED, plus an **authority-weight** column: whether each side of a conflict is inside the FRAMEWORK §14.7 authority surface — "`lifecycle == Live` **OR** (`lifecycle == LOCKED` **AND** `tier ∈ {1, 2}`)" (`FRAMEWORK.md` §14.7). A contradiction between two authority-surface members is graver than one between an authoritative document and a Draft.

**Out of scope.** Re-running the prior code review; patching any existing document (recommendations are recorded in §8 for a follow-up session); the 25 pre-existing register orphan warnings (baseline re-verified: `sync_register.ps1 -Validate` → exit 0, 25 advisory warnings, 291 documents).

Of the 27 authoritative docs, only 8 are cleanly CURRENT; 4 are TARGET (including three LOCKED gameplay protocols and the dormant FHE contract); the rest are MIXED. **The single most consequential mismatch:** the canonical К-L12/К-L15 texts sit in a non-authoritative document while five authoritative documents cite them as law (§6.1, N-1).

## 3. External review verdicts: the 12 contradictions

Verdict + authority-weight for each. Quotes verbatim; doc anchors by section, code anchors as `file:line`.

| # | Verdict | Authority-weight |
|---|---|---|
| C1 | **CONFIRMED** | Both sides authoritative (KERNEL, THREADING, KFNS all LOCKED T1) |
| C2 | **CONFIRMED** (nuance: EVENT_BUS itself is honest) | Both sides authoritative |
| C3 | **CONFIRMED** | Single LOCKED doc, internally |
| C4 | **CONFIRMED** | Single LOCKED doc, internally |
| C5 | **CONFIRMED as text, RECLASSIFIED in severity** | Stale side is Draft → **not** authoritative; authority surface is internally consistent on this point |
| C6 | **CONFIRMED** | Both sides authoritative (FIELDS Live vs VULKAN LOCKED) |
| C7 | **CONFIRMED** | Single LOCKED doc, internally |
| C8 | **CONFIRMED** as multi-doc incoherence; compile-time reality is currently clean | Authoritative docs disagree among themselves |
| C9 | **REFUTED as stated** (review mischaracterized the umbrella); residual design concern stands | — |
| C10 | **CONFIRMED**, origin is deeper than claimed | Both sides authoritative |
| C11 | **CONFIRMED** with honesty nuance | Both sides authoritative |
| C12 | **CONFIRMED and amplified** (the CI gate itself is fictional) | Single LOCKED doc vs reality |

### C1 — dual scheduling authority. CONFIRMED.

Doc side: К-L12 sovereignty — "Native kernel owns sovereign per-tick scheduling for kernel-space systems (Core)… dependency graph construction, runqueue maintenance, wake-up dispatch, phase composition, parallelism scheduling, priority arbitration, resource quota enforcement" (`KERNEL_FULL_NATIVE_SCHEDULER.md` §2.1; abbreviated row `KERNEL_ARCHITECTURE.md` Part 0). Production truth, stated by THREADING itself: the bootstrap "registers every Core system **twice**"; native registration "passes empty read/write component-id sets … and a TimerWake at rate 1 — every system is woken every tick"; managed `DependencyGraph` "builds write-to-read edges … groups systems into ordered `SystemPhase` lists"; execution via `Parallel.ForEach`; the batched-callback adapter "is on disk and test-exercised… Production per-phase dispatch does not yet route through it — the switch is Planned" (`THREADING.md` §The managed layer).

Code: `GameBootstrap.cs:145-181` (dual registration, empty sets at :170-176, `SubscribeTimer(i,1)` at :179); `ParallelSystemScheduler.cs:149` (`Parallel.ForEach`), driven by `GameLoop.cs:115`; adapter `ManagedSystemDispatcher.cs:75` / `SchedulerAdapter.cs:22` invoked **only** from tests (`BatchedCallbackTests.cs:30`).

Aggravating findings the review lacked: (a) `KERNEL_FULL_NATIVE_SCHEDULER.md` §4.1 asserts "К10.1–К10.3 shipped (native scheduler live…)" and `K_CLOSURE_REPORT.md:508-514` lists the native scheduler files under "Production manifestation" with **no unwired caveat** — evidence docs present К-L12 as achieved while the normative THREADING says otherwise; (b) **no document names a cutover gate** — the only cutover condition found anywhere is a code comment (`GameBootstrap.cs:157-159`); (c) no deletion trigger exists for the managed `DependencyGraph` (KFNS Item 1 says "Replace managed `DependencyGraph.cs`" with no retirement criterion); (d) the К-L14 evidence dashboard contains **zero** scheduler-cutover verification rows, versus three for the bus (#2, #8, #9) — the asymmetry is measurable.

### C2 — dual event routing. CONFIRMED, with an honesty nuance.

Doc side: К-L15 — "Native bus authority: native kernel owns sovereign event routing для kernel-space and cross-layer events… type registry, subscriber registry, payload dispatch, wake firing, tier-based delivery semantics all native authority" (`K_CLOSURE_REPORT.md:577`, canonical carrier; tag rows in KERNEL Part 0). Production truth, stated by EVENT_BUS itself: the managed path is "the dispatch path production events actually travel today"; "the flag defaults to `false`. No production code constructs a `BusFacade`"; the sovereign switch is "Planned — see docs/ROADMAP.md" (`EVENT_BUS.md` §Division of labor).

Code: five domain buses wired in production (`GameServices.cs:16-113`, constructed `GameBootstrap.cs:79`); `BusFacade.cs:49` `UseNativeBusForDispatch { get; set; } = false`, instantiated only in tests; live native touchpoints today are exactly two — Background-tier drain (`ManagedBusBridge.DrainBackgroundBatch`, `GameLoop.cs:120-128`) and per-mod bulk unsubscribe on unload ("vacuously today, since production registers no native subscribers", `EVENT_BUS.md` §Division of labor).

Nuance: unlike the scheduler cluster, `EVENT_BUS.md` v2.0.0 is *honest* current-truth — the contradiction is between the К-L15 "sovereign" label and near-zero production traffic, an authority-vs-traffic split that invites misreading rather than a doc lying about wiring. Novel: observability is inverted — the dormant native path has a full diagnostics ABI while "The managed `DomainEventBus` keeps no counters" (`EVENT_BUS.md` §Diagnostics).

### C3 — Rule 5 vs К-L12 reverse-P/Invoke. CONFIRMED.

"**Rule 5**: Native side has no callbacks к managed. Direction unidirectional (managed → native always)." (`KERNEL_ARCHITECTURE.md:319`, §1.5 LOCKED invariants) and Part 8: "managed → native always, never reverse P/Invoke" (`KERNEL_ARCHITECTURE.md:897`) — versus Part 0 К-L12: "Cross-layer communication uses C ABI with batched callbacks (managed adapter receives batched managed-system dispatches from kernel scheduler)". Grep confirms **zero in-document reconciliation**. The de-facto reconciliation exists only in `THREADING.md:43` (the `[UnmanagedCallersOnly]` trampoline described as "К-L12 bridge") — i.e., a different document quietly resolves what the owning document contradicts. `K_CLOSURE_REPORT.md:501` asserts batched callbacks as settled with no Rule 5 reference. The review's proposed fix (restate Rule 5 as "callbacks only through the registered batched C ABI trampoline") is the correct amendment.

### C4 — KERNEL §1.4 stale threading model. CONFIRMED.

§1.4 verbatim: "Thread pool idle (managed scheduler doesn't use it)"; "Native World single-threaded для writes. Managed scheduler ensures only one phase active at a time"; "Worker threads (existing ParallelSystemScheduler)". This is pre-К10.1 text standing unamended in a LOCKED doc whose own Part 0 assigns native "runqueue maintenance, wake-up dispatch, phase composition, parallelism scheduling". R1 catalogued six self-contradiction pairs in KERNEL alone, including Part 4 Open "Native event bus… Currently не planned (К-L6 keeps systems managed)" vs Part 0 К-L15 LOCKED, and closing notes "This document is **v1.5** (current)" vs frontmatter `version: 2.6.2`. The structural diagnosis (new, §6.1 N-2): Part 1 (§1.3–§1.9) and Part 8 were never rewritten after К10.x — **two normative layers coexist in one LOCKED document with no precedence marker**. KFNS adds a live ambiguity: Item 15 leaves the managed batch executor's parallelism policy explicitly unresolved ("In-batch parallelism via TPL… Or sequential within batch").

### C5 — DEBUG runtime guard. CONFIRMED as text; RECLASSIFIED in severity.

`ARCHITECTURE_TYPE_SYSTEM.md` asserts the guard exists in at least nine places, most centrally: "Once the analyzer ships, the DEBUG runtime guard remains in place — it is not redundant." and "The runtime guard's RELEASE-mode elision (`#if DEBUG` in `SystemExecutionContext.GetComponent<T>` / `SetComponent<T>`) is unaffected by this specification." (§2.4). Reality: "The runtime guard methods that previously threw `IsolationViolationException` … were **deleted in K8.3+K8.4 cutover (A'.5 closure 2026-05-14)**" (`ISOLATION.md` §Enforcement model; corroborated by `THREADING.md` §Execution contexts, `MIGRATION_PLAN` §1.3; code: `SystemAccessAttribute.cs:11-14`, `SystemExecutionContext.cs:24-31`, no `#if DEBUG` in scheduler/ECS code).

Reclassification: TYPE_SYSTEM is **Draft** — outside the §14.7 authority surface. The authoritative documents are mutually consistent here. So this is not a Tier-1-vs-Tier-1 contradiction as the review framed it; it is a stale Draft plus a much sharper problem the review missed: the **analyzer program schism** (§6.1, N-13) — the Draft family's anchoring consumer no longer exists, its v0.1.1 patch (2026-06-12, a month *after* the deletion) says "specification content unchanged", and the actually-shipped analyzer regime follows none of the Draft family's laws.

### C6 — FIELDS transparent CPU/GPU vs VULKAN fail-fast. CONFIRMED.

FIELDS: "The runtime decides whether DispatchCompute issues a Vulkan compute dispatch or runs the CPU reference kernel. The mod doesn't care." (§CPU and GPU paths); "The storage path is required to function on CPU alone"; it even cites a VULKAN anchor that no longer exists: "per VULKAN_SUBSTRATE «Failure modes → CPU fallback»". VULKAN: "The shipped policy resolves this by exclusion, not fallback" and "a config-selected per-tick CPU execution path for fields exists only as design; no fallback dispatcher is on disk" (§7.1); К-L19 fail-fast "throws HardwareCapabilityException if the Vulkan 1.3 + async-compute-queue hardware tier is absent". Code: `VulkanInstance.cs:26-40`, `HardwareCapabilityCheck.cs:23`; `CpuKernels/*` are explicitly "Equivalence oracle … **не** as a performance target". Both docs are on the authority surface — a genuine surface-internal contradiction. The review's resolution (keep the 1.3 floor, fix FIELDS) is right; note FIELDS also pins "VULKAN_SUBSTRATE — v2.0 LOCKED" (actual: v1.2.0) and superseded G-series numbering throughout (§6.3, N-24).

### C7 — MOD_OS atomic vs best-effort unload. CONFIRMED.

"Transitions between states are atomic; failure mid-transition rolls back to the previous state." (§9) versus "There is no atomic-unload guarantee. `Unload` is conceptually irreversible: subscriptions removed in step 1 cannot be re-attached without re-running `Subscribe`." (§9.5.1) — same LOCKED document. The state list is Disabled/Pending/Loaded/Active/Stopping; **no Degraded state**, and the fault path (systems "marked `Disabled` in the scheduler" while the ALC stays loaded, `MOD_PIPELINE` §ModFaultHandler) is unrepresentable in the §9.1 diagram. Worst case is explicit in-doc: on step-7 timeout "the mod is marked as a leaked reference and the user is advised to restart" — i.e., a "Disabled" display can coexist with a live leaked assembly. Code: `ModIntegrationPipeline.cs:209` (Apply with rollback), `:561` (best-effort 6-step unload), `EditableModInfo.cs:31` (boolean flags, no state enum). The review's split (atomic desired-state commit vs non-atomic reclamation state machine) is exactly right and is the core of the `ENGINE_LIFECYCLE_AND_TRANSACTIONS.md` draft (§7).

### C8 — "mods reference only Contracts". CONFIRMED as documented incoherence; the compile-time surface is currently clean.

The four documents disagree among themselves: `ARCHITECTURE.md` §Layer map: "Mods reference **only** Contracts." — with the row claiming this is "enforced by `AssemblyLoadContext` isolation". `CONTRACTS.md` §IModContract sanctions separate inter-mod assemblies: "`ModName.Contracts` assembly, published as a NuGet package or sitting alongside in the `mods/` directory". `MOD_OS_ARCHITECTURE.md` §3.5 builds the kernel capability surface by "a reflection scan over `DualFrontier.Contracts`, `DualFrontier.Components`, and `DualFrontier.Events`", and its §2.1 manifest example requires naming those types (`kernel.read:DualFrontier.Components.Combat.WeaponComponent`). `MODDING.md` lists `DualFrontier.Components`/`Events` as **refused** by the mod ALC — yet its own quickstart calls `api.Subscribe<DeathEvent>(OnDeath)` where `DeathEvent` is `DualFrontier.Events.Combat.DeathEvent`, a type unreachable under those rules; the doc's example cannot compile under the doc's own law.

Code reality (V1, new evidence): all 7 mod csprojs and every test fixture reference **only** `DualFrontier.Contracts` (e.g. `mods/…/Example.csproj:8`, `Fixture.SharedEvents.csproj:10`); the only extra reference anywhere is fixture→fixture (`Fixture.PublisherMod.csproj:13-15`, the shared-mod test pattern); **no** `SharedModContracts`-style assembly exists; no mod source imports Components/Events. So the review's claim that concrete kernel types sit in the mod dependency surface is TRUE at the documented-contract level (capabilities, type-sharing protocol, examples) and currently FALSE at the compile level — which makes this a documentation-coherence defect, fixable by pinning the exact Mod SDK assembly set, precisely as the review recommended.

### C9 — "Domain doesn't know native" vs Components → Core.Interop. REFUTED as stated.

`ARCHITECTURE.md` contains **no such rule**. The Domain box says only "game rules; multithreaded; renderer-agnostic"; the dependency law is "Dependency direction is strictly downward in the layer map; Presentation never calls Domain directly" (§Dependency rules) — which *legalizes* Domain→Infrastructure edges; and the table itself prints the row "Components | Contracts, Core.Interop" as csproj-verified truth (`DualFrontier.Components.csproj:11`; carries `InternedString`, `NativeMap` in 6 files). There is no intra-document contradiction to fix. What survives is a *design judgment* the review argued under a wrong premise: whether domain data structs should reference the interop assembly at all. That question is real (the `Kernel.Abstractions` extraction remains a sound proposal — §8, AD-4-amended) but it is not a Tier-1 truth defect. One review sub-claim is also corrected by R5: the umbrella's own tension is different — its intro claims "event routing are native authority" while its Layer map houses "domain buses" in managed Core with no boundary rule (see §6.1).

### C10 — ECS teaches `EntityId(indices[i], 0)`. CONFIRMED; the origin is the kernel doc itself.

`ECS.md` §Span/Batch canonical loop: `var id = new EntityId(indices[i], 0);` against its own law — "The version is incremented when an entity is destroyed, which makes old references safely invalid" (§EntityId); "An entity with an old version is the indicator of a dead reference" (§Versioning). New: the pattern originates in `KERNEL_ARCHITECTURE.md` §1.7 — "new EntityId(lease.Indices[i], 0); // version not exposed via span" — the kernel authority doc seeds the identity bug its dependents repeat. Code scale (V3): ten systems plus `EntityEncoder.cs:85` fabricate version-0 ids; `SpanLease.cs:76-84,112` fabricates version **1** ("SpanLease does not currently track per-entity versions"). Native enforcement exists and is correct — `world.cpp:74-78` `is_alive` requires `id.version == versions_[id.index]`, `destroy_entity` bumps the version — but managed fabrication collapses the ABA guarantee to "index has never been recycled". A latent micro-drift compounds it: managed `EntityId.IsValid => Index > 0 || Version > 0` (`EntityId.cs:38`) accepts ids native `is_alive` rejects. Resolution is specified in the `IDENTITY_AND_ABI_CONTRACT.md` draft (§7): the span ABI must surface true versions; the teaching examples must be amended.

### C11 — COMBO_RESOLUTION Tier-1 LOCKED vs stub. CONFIRMED, with an honesty nuance.

The determinism promise is real: "the resulting `DamageEvent` stream is byte-identical between runs" (§Determinism guarantee) — and the system is `NotImplementedException` (`ComboResolutionSystem.cs:60,76`; `CompositeResolutionSystem` has six stubs). But the doc discloses it prominently: "currently exists as a **stub that throws `NotImplementedException`**" and self-declares "design specification, NON-NORMATIVE… not implemented behavior" (§Implementation status, banner verified-against-code 2026-06-02). The paradox is *governance-shaped*: a LOCKED Tier-1 document that disclaims its own normativity. Same pattern in COMPOSITE_REQUESTS; **RESOURCE_MODELS lacks the honesty banner entirely** (unqualified present tense over a partially-implemented surface) — the worse instance, unremarked by the review. The determinism-class conflict stands at surface level: VULKAN §7.2.1 "Realtime simulation does not require bit-exact determinism (single-player, no replays)" vs COMBO's byte-identical replay promise, both authoritative. Resolution vocabulary (D0/D1/D2 classes) is specified in the `TIME_AND_CONSISTENCY_MODEL.md` draft (§7).

### C12 — PERFORMANCE.md staleness. CONFIRMED and amplified.

The review said the doc measures the wrong substrate. V2's symbol-by-symbol sweep proves more: the enforcement the doc pins **does not exist**. "These values are pinned in CI through `PerformanceGate` — tests fail when regression exceeds 10%" (§Target metrics) and "In `tests/DualFrontier.Core.Benchmarks/PerformanceGates.cs` add a rule" (§How to add) — zero matches repo-wide for either; there is **no `.github/` directory and no CI configuration of any kind**; the benchmark suite states "no pass/fail invariants; these benchmarks give numbers" (`SchedulerStressBenchmarks.cs:15`). Additional decay: `SpatialGrid.QueryRadius` renamed (`GetInRadius`, `SpatialGrid.cs:127`); `PositionChangedEvent` replaced by `PawnMovedEvent` (`PawnMovedEvent.cs:9`); `ComponentStoreBenchmark` gone; `Dictionary<ItemType,…>` now `Dictionary<int,List<string>>` (`InventorySystem.cs:27`); the "Isolation violation → Crash + diagnostics via SystemExecutionContext" row asserts the deleted runtime guard; two conflicting `Get` budgets (5 ns §Hot paths vs ≤10 ns §How to add); only the ComponentStore section carries a supersession fence. Accurate residue: `AStarPathfinding` 2000-iteration cap and `TryFindPath` semantics verified exact (`AStarPathfinding.cs:14,21`).

## 4. External review verdicts: the 9 missing cross-cutting contracts

Two evidence streams joined per contract: positive fragments (readers' [A-tag] extraction across all 32 docs) and an absence sweep (V2 keyword sets over `docs/architecture/` + `docs/governance/`).

| A# | Verdict | Strongest existing fragments | Decisive absence evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| A0 execution authority matrix | **CONFIRMED missing** | К-L12/К-L15 rows; ARCHITECTURE intro sentence; VULKAN Preamble authority statement; MOD_OS Preamble; ISOLATION 3-stage enforcement | No matrix anywhere; §14.7 predicate has **no inter-doc precedence rule**; event-routing has two owners inside the umbrella itself; persistence & entity-identity are ownerless at umbrella level (R5) |
| A1 concurrency/memory model | **CONFIRMED missing** | EVENT_BUS:64 fixed mutex order fast→normal→background; THREADING barrier + MaxDoP; FIELDS mutation-rejection; К-L11 declaration-based no-write-write; KFNS TLA+ (pending) | V2 sweep: **zero hits corpus-wide** for happens-before / memory model / lock order / data race / Interlocked / memory barrier |
| A2 ownership/lifetime graph | **CONFIRMED missing** | К-L8; FIELDS span lifetime + back-buffer law; EVENT_BUS lease model + subscription lifecycle; MOD_OS ALC rules; VULKAN handle ownership | No ownership tree, no dispose-order law, no `EngineSession`; proven costly: shutdown gap cascade (§6.3, N-19) |
| A3 lifecycle/transaction model | **PARTIALLY** — fragments real, vocabulary absent | MOD_PIPELINE Apply is genuine prepare/commit ("graph built on a local; scheduler replaced only after Build() succeeds"); MIGRATION_PLAN §5 atomic-commit-set + revert-as-unit; К-L18 quiesce protocol; KFNS Item 32 single-critical-section unload | No shared prepare/validate/quiesce/commit/reclaim/recover vocabulary; C7 is the visible symptom; mod-fault timing specified 3 different ways (N-8); swapchain recreation shipped with no transactional protocol; world shutdown has no transaction at all |
| A4 time/consistency model | **PARTIALLY** — strong local rules, no unified model | К-L16/К-L7.1 slot-tail law; FEEDBACK_LOOPS previous-tick snapshot rule (normative); ECS phase-boundary visibility; EVENT_BUS deferred-flush semantics incl. queue-snapshot drain | No unified visibility table; vocabulary diverges per doc; FIELDS "field dispatches are non-blocking" directly contradicts VULKAN's К-L7 sync path ("returns after fence signals"); determinism classes undefined (C11) |
| A5 identity/registry contract | **PARTIALLY** — rich fragments, live drift | KERNEL §1.10 type-id law ("0 reserved for invalid", idempotent, load-order-deterministic); FIELDS string ids with reload-stability rationale; MOD_OS ModId + capability grammar + 3-axis SemVer; EVENT_BUS subscription-id bit layout; FNV-1a event ids | No unified contract; three live drifts: version-0 idiom (C10), VULKAN §3.4 `uint32_t field_id` sketch vs shipped `const char*` ABI, managed `IsValid` vs native `is_alive`; FNV collision policy absent |
| A6 C ABI & error contract | **CONFIRMED missing** | KERNEL:759 status-code + `catch (...)` swallow-all law; Part 7 four-category managed rule; KFNS Item 15 blittable/`SuppressGCTransition` constraints | V2 sweep: 4 hit-docs only; no ABI version negotiation, no struct layout law, no buffer-ownership rules, no error taxonomy (retryable/fatal/contract-violation), no per-entry-point thread affinity, no pointer validity windows |
| A7 persistence snapshot contract | **CONFIRMED missing** | FIELDS blob sketch (back buffer not serialized; resize = load error); К-L3.1 Path β runtime-only; KFNS Item 31 background-queue persistence "versioned wire format, schema v1"; MOD_OS D-6 missing-mod policy; FHE D7 reserved 4th version field | Everything else deferred; `DualFrontier.Persistence` project is **orphaned from production** (only tests reference it; "SaveSystem currently stub" `PipelineSlotInterop.cs:202`; deliberate per MIGRATION_PLAN §8.1); `EntityEncoder.cs:68` "Returns entities with version 0" — generations not serialized; sequential TypeIds are load-order-dependent, unaddressed for saves |
| A8 fault/recovery/observability | **CONFIRMED missing** as a unified model | ISOLATION two response modes (Core→crash, Mod→unload); К-L19 fail-fast; KFNS Item 7 quota→ModFaultHandler + Item 20 intrinsics; native bus saturation counters; per-mod fault logs | No failure taxonomy; **no device-lost story anywhere** (V2: zero hits; R3: NOT-FOUND in VULKAN); no Degraded state in code; DEBUG-throw/RELEASE-silent forks unjustified (OWNERSHIP_TRANSITION); managed bus keeps no counters |

The review's A0–A8 list is therefore fully validated, with one amendment it could not see: **A0 partially pre-exists in spirit nowhere** — the review implicitly treated `OWNERSHIP_TRANSITION.md` as unexamined; R2 confirmed it is a *gameplay* golem-bond protocol whose title merely collides with architecture vocabulary (§6.1, N-6), so no A0 content hides there. The seven draft documents in §7 are this session's response; A8 is distributed rather than standalone (taxonomy+recovery → lifecycle draft; error classes → ABI draft; correlation IDs → authority-matrix draft).

## 5. Doc↔code conformance matrix

Aggregate verdict per document over its checkable CODE-CLAIMS (readers) joined with code probes (V1–V3). Categories: HOLDS (claims verified), STALE (claims about code that has moved), BROKEN (claims that were never true or contradict reality), UNBUILT (claims about admittedly future surface — not a defect when fenced).

| Document | Verdict | Decisive evidence |
|---|---|---|
| THREADING.md | **HOLDS** (exemplary) | Every wiring claim verified: dual registration `GameBootstrap.cs:145-181`, empty sets, `system_graph.cpp` symbols, adapter test-only, MaxDoP N-2 `ParallelSystemScheduler.cs:90`. One reverse-staleness: "17 rule stubs… non-detecting" predates A'.9.1 — detection now real (see ANALYZER_RULES row) |
| EVENT_BUS.md | **HOLDS** | Five-bus canon (`IGameServices.cs`), flag=false + no production `BusFacade` construction, Background drain wired `GameLoop.cs:120-128`, native 4-TU split present, S10 probe exists |
| ECS.md | **HOLDS**, teaching example harmful | API surface verified; the §Span/Batch example fabricates version-0 ids (C10); comment "Access is permitted — both components are declared in reads" implies a runtime check that was deleted |
| KERNEL_ARCHITECTURE.md | Part 0 **HOLDS** / Parts 1,4,8 **STALE** | §1.4 pool-idle/single-threaded-writes; §1.3 managed-driven tick; Part 4 "native bus… не planned"; Part 8 "never reverse P/Invoke"; closing "v1.5" vs 2.6.2; §1.7 seeds version-0 idiom |
| PERFORMANCE.md | **STALE + BROKEN enforcement** | `PerformanceGate`/`PerformanceGates.cs` nonexistent; no CI at all; `QueryRadius`→`GetInRadius`; `PositionChangedEvent`→`PawnMovedEvent`; `ComponentStoreBenchmark` gone; isolation row asserts deleted guard. Accurate: A* cap 2000 (`AStarPathfinding.cs:14`) |
| ARCHITECTURE.md | **HOLDS** | Dependency table == csproj truth (verified rows incl. `Components | Contracts, Core.Interop`); 12-project census correct |
| CONTRACTS.md | **HOLDS**, one stale marker | Five-bus surface real; "future A'.9 Roslyn analyzer" predates shipped analyzers |
| FIELDS.md | Storage **HOLDS** / GPU-path **UNBUILT+STALE** | `tile_field.h/cpp`, `FieldRegistry/FieldHandle` verified; `DispatchCompute`/`Pipelines.Get` surface returns null today (`RestrictedModApi.ComputePipelines` hardwired null); superseded G-numbering; "VULKAN v2.0 LOCKED" pin (actual 1.2.0) |
| VULKAN_SUBSTRATE.md | **HOLDS** with internal drift | Fail-fast verified (`VulkanInstance.cs:26-40`); CpuKernels-as-oracle verified; §3.4 `uint32_t field_id` sketch contradicts its own shipped `const char*` bridge; input drain-and-discard admission verified (`Program.cs:81-84`) |
| MOD_OS / MOD_PIPELINE / MODDING / ISOLATION | Individually **HOLDS**, mutually inconsistent | ALC collectible + Apply rollback + best-effort unload verified (`ModIntegrationPipeline.cs:209,561`); but unload step order differs MOD_OS vs MOD_PIPELINE; fault timing differs 3 ways; `ValidationErrorKind` claimed 15 members (MOD_OS) vs 4 (MOD_PIPELINE) for the same file; MODDING examples fail own v3 schema |
| MIGRATION_PLAN | **HOLDS** with stale arithmetic | `ManagedTestWorld` fixture, SaveSystem deliberate stub verified; system counts contradict themselves (§1.2 "10" vs "31"; deleted-systems 2 vs 3); dangling internal ref §0.3 #4 |
| ARCHITECTURE_TYPE_SYSTEM.md (Draft) | **BROKEN** | Nine claims about a runtime guard deleted 2026-05-14; `DF_TS_*` ids nonexistent; `src/DualFrontier.Analyzers/` path never created (shipped: `tools/`) |
| TRACK_B_ACTIVATION.md (Draft) | **BROKEN** | Anchors on deleted `SystemAccessRules.cs` predicate symbols; pre-activation invariant "No analyzer code is written" falsified by A'.9.1; trigger event ("K9 closure = K-series closure") no longer denotes the settled-corpus event after Option-c reordering |
| ANALYZER_RULES.md (SKELETON) | **HOLDS** (best current-truth among non-authoritative) | 17 implemented rule IDs verified 1:1 (V2); `Directory.Build.props:30-33` wiring to all 12 src projects verified; 2 DFK-WAIVERs in `ValidationLayer.cs` verified; 13 declared-not-implemented IDs are explicitly deferred, not overclaimed |
| COMBO / COMPOSITE | **UNBUILT (self-admitted)** | Stubs verified `ComboResolutionSystem.cs:60,76`; banners honest |
| RESOURCE_MODELS | **PARTIALLY** — unbannered | Lease registry/`ManaSystem` surface partially on disk; `TargetIntent` trio uncorroborated; no implementation-status banner |
| OWNERSHIP_TRANSITION | **HOLDS** | All five types verified present; absent pieces (`GolemOwnershipRefused`, `GolemBondStrength`) correctly flagged TODO |
| FHE_INTEGRATION_CONTRACT | **HOLDS** (dormant) | `DualFrontier.Crypto.Future` = 2 empty reserved interfaces, zero consumers — exactly as contracted |
| FRAMEWORK / PROJECT_AXIOMS / SYNTHESIS_RATIONALE | **HOLDS** with self-referential defects | Governance tooling exists as claimed; but FRAMEWORK 2.0 header vs REGISTER schema 1.0 breaches its own §8.2 rule 4 during the declared transition window; SYNTHESIS_RATIONALE lockstep promise unmet; PA-001 line-anchor broken (line 21→27) |
| K_CLOSURE / A'.9 RECON / DASHBOARD / LEDGER | **HOLDS as evidence**, overstated as status | K_CLOSURE §2.14 "Production manifestation" lists scheduler files with no unwired caveat — contradicts THREADING; ledger §3.5 "Closed" entry with TBD commit hashes |

**Systemic pattern.** Documents rewritten under the "code-truth" discipline (THREADING, EVENT_BUS, ARCHITECTURE, ANALYZER_RULES, MOD_OS v1.12 sections) hold almost perfectly. Documents that predate a cutover and were never swept (KERNEL Parts 1/4/8, PERFORMANCE, FIELDS GPU sections, both Drafts) carry every serious conformance defect found. The variable that predicts truthfulness is not tier or lifecycle — it is whether the doc went through a truth-scrub after the last substrate change.

## 6. New findings of this session

Numbered N-1…N-27, grouped. Each finding names its evidence; none of these appear in the external review.

### 6.1 Authority-surface pathologies (governance layer)

- **N-1. The canonical К-L12/К-L15 texts live outside the authority surface.** `K_CLOSURE_REPORT.md` (Tier 1 **AUTHORED** → fails §14.7) carries the fullest normative wording of both invariants (§2.14 line 497, §2.17 line 577), self-designates as "full canonical text as load-bearing authority surface" for К-L14 (line 76), and A'.9 recon defers to it as canonical (`A_PRIME_9_RECONNAISSANCE_REPORT.md:574,648`). KERNEL Part 0 keeps abbreviated rows and explicitly delegates К-L14's canonical text there. An agent honoring the authority predicate must *ignore* the very document that defines the law.
- **N-2. KERNEL_ARCHITECTURE contains two normative layers with no precedence marker.** Part 0 (post-К10 law) vs §1.3–§1.9/Part 4/Part 8 (pre-К10 spec never rewritten): six intra-doc contradiction pairs catalogued (C3, C4, plus "v1.5" closing note, Part 4 native-bus denial, §1.9 registration flow, Part 8 diagram).
- **N-3. §14.7 has no inter-document precedence rule, and one branch is unpopulated.** Nothing arbitrates when two authority-surface members claim the same domain (live example: ARCHITECTURE "Mods reference **only** Contracts" vs CONTRACTS.md sanctioning `ModName.Contracts` assemblies). Additionally the `LOCKED AND tier 2` branch has no legal population under the §3.4 category-tier matrix — predicate and matrix disagree (R5).
- **N-4. The register's own lockstep laws are currently breached.** FRAMEWORK header at 2.0.0 while `REGISTER.yaml` `schema_version: "1.0"` breaches FRAMEWORK's still-operative §8.2 rule 4 (its §7.3 even mandates halt-on-mismatch); `SYNTHESIS_RATIONALE.md` §0/§6 lockstep promise unamended; `PROJECT_AXIOMS` PA-001 cites "FRAMEWORK.md §0 / line 21" which now sits at line 27 — a LOCKED-doc line anchor broke silently. The §14.9 dual-regime declaration covers FRAMEWORK's own sections, not these cross-doc promises.
- **N-5. Frontmatter mirrors will invert into falsehoods at Cascade B.** Every mirror says "Auto-generated from docs/governance/REGISTER.yaml — DO NOT EDIT MANUALLY"; under the §14.1 target law the frontmatter *becomes* the source of truth (R5).
- **N-6. `OWNERSHIP_TRANSITION.md` is a gameplay protocol wearing an architecture title.** Verified: mage–golem bond state machine (sole architecture fragment: one single-writer rule). At Tier 1 LOCKED in `docs/architecture/`, its name collides with A2 vocabulary — high misclassification risk for any authority audit (the external review's silence about it was *materially* justified, accidentally).
- **N-7. Honesty banners are inconsistent across the gameplay-protocol family.** COMBO/COMPOSITE carry "design specification, NON-NORMATIVE" banners; **RESOURCE_MODELS does not** — unqualified present tense over a partially-implemented surface; FEEDBACK_LOOPS carries a self-flagged unverified enforcement claim (`IsolationViolationException` at registration, DD-1 note).
- **N-8. Mod-fault unload timing is specified three different ways** across LOCKED docs: MOD_OS §10.1 "queued and unloaded … at the next menu open"; MOD_PIPELINE §ModFaultHandler chain-now-rebuild-deferred; ISOLATION §ModFaultHandler immediate full sequence with a step-4 abort-on-timeout semantic stronger than MOD_OS §9.5.1's log-and-continue.
- **N-9. MOD_OS vs MOD_PIPELINE unload chains disagree in step order and content** (Unsubscribe-first vs RemoveSystems-first; §9.5 omits `IMod.Unload()` although §9.5.1 cites it as canonical discipline; MOD_PIPELINE labels ClearManagedStores "MOD_OS §9.5 chain step 5" — that step is the scheduler swap). `ValidationErrorKind`: 15 members claimed (MOD_OS §11.2) vs 4 quoted (MOD_PIPELINE) for the same file.
- **N-10. MODDING.md examples fail the schema it defers to**: `requiresContracts` vs `requiresContractsVersion`, `optionalDependencies`, and capability `kernel.bus.subscribe:Magic` — a form that violates MOD_OS §2.3 grammar and the §3.1 per-type LOCK.
- **N-11. Capability grammar drift inside and across docs**: MOD_OS §3.2 lists `layer.intent`/`layer.combat_feedback` verbs while §2.3 states "layer.* is not part of this manifest grammar"; FIELDS uses two incompatible capability string shapes in different sections.
- **N-12. K_EXTENSIONS_LEDGER convention breaks**: `version: "Live"` (breaks sibling semver convention), mixed-language prose in a Tier-2 Live artifact, and a "Closed 2026-05-24" cascade entry whose commit list still reads TBD.
- **N-13. The analyzer program schism.** Two disjoint programs: (a) Draft DF_TS family — `ARCHITECTURE_TYPE_SYSTEM.md` (severity Error-only §3.1, suppressions forbidden §3.2, "CI fails the build on any emission", claims MOD_OS capability model is "anchored here") + `TRACK_B_ACTIVATION` (claims closure authority over LOCKED MOD_OS's M3.4 via its own §4.3 — a Draft closing a LOCKED doc's milestone); (b) shipped DFK/DFL/DF999 regime — `ANALYZER_RULES.md` (17 real rules, Warning tiers, 2 sanctioned DFK-WAIVERs, `tools/` location). Neither cites the other; the shipped regime structurally violates every law the Draft family legislates; the Draft family's anchoring consumer (runtime guard) is deleted; and `MIGRATION_PLAN` (LOCKED) normatively depends on "the future A'.9 Roslyn analyzer to catch undeclared accesses" — a `[SystemAccess]`-completeness rule that exists in **neither** program's shipped registry.
- **N-14. The KERNEL header chronicle is a shadow decision register**: DFK002 federated interop model, analyzer severities, waiver decisions live only in a version-history paragraph (~line 17), findable in no body section.
- **N-15. KFNS lock provenance is unverifiable in-repo**: "K10_DELIBERATION_STATE (external deliberation archive — not in repository)" holds the lock rationale for a Tier-1 LOCKED doc (§0.5/Part 11); its Q-N-3 record still says the deliberation *assumed* "Bus stays managed authority (status quo)" — never reconciled with К-L15.

### 6.2 Undocumented and orphaned subsystems

- **N-16. The AI subsystem is ungoverned.** `src/DualFrontier.AI` (Pathfinding, BehaviourTree, Jobs) is consumed in production (MovementSystem, GameBootstrap, factories — Pathfinding namespace only) yet no architecture document governs it — it appears only in a glossary, a housekeeping prompt, and a rename backlog. **BehaviourTree and Jobs have zero external consumers** — a dead or dormant surface shipping in the production dependency graph (V1).
- **N-17. `DualFrontier.Persistence` is orphaned from production.** Snapshots (PawnSnapshot, WorldSnapshot, …) and compression codecs exist, referenced **only by its test project**; `SaveSystem` stub is deliberate (MIGRATION_PLAN §8.1) but no doc records that a whole assembly ships disconnected (V1).
- **N-18. Input→simulation path does not exist.** The Launcher drains `InputQueue` and discards every event ("Future cascade will forward", `Program.cs:81-84`); no contract documents input routing, focus→pause coupling is unwired (VULKAN §2.2 admission verified). The presentation direction is contract-clean (one-way bridge) but the queue is unbounded (`PresentationBridge.cs:21`).

### 6.3 Contract gaps beyond A0–A8

- **N-19. Shutdown gap cascade** (V3, the session's most consequential code-side finding): (1) `NativeWorld` is a local in `GameBootstrap.CreateLoop` (`GameBootstrap.cs:76`), **not** carried by `GameContext` (`GameContext.cs:19`) — `df_world_destroy` is never called from Application/Launcher; disposal happens only at GC finalization (`NativeWorld.cs:486-500`); (2) `GameLoop.Stop` = CTS cancel + `Thread.Join(2000)` — on timeout the sim thread is **abandoned** while possibly inside `ExecuteTick` touching NativeWorld (`GameLoop.cs:73-77`), racing finalization; (3) no native scheduler teardown on shutdown (`SystemGraphInterop.Clear` only at bootstrap start); (4) no native bus teardown (`df_bus_clear` reachable only via `ClearForTesting`), subscriber GCHandles never freed; (5) `ModIntegrationPipeline.UnloadAll` (`ModIntegrationPipeline.cs:780`) has **zero callers**; (6) `gameContext` is never disposed. The only real quiesce waits in shutdown are the two Vulkan `WaitIdle` calls.
- **N-20. No configuration contract.** Zero config files/loaders; everything is hardcoded consts: map 200×200 (`GameBootstrap.cs:58-59`), seeds 42/43, spawn counts, `TargetTps=30` (`GameLoop.cs:29`), catch-up clamp, window 1280×720, `maxSpritesPerFrame:10_000`, camera assuming a 200×200 map (`LauncherRenderer.cs:36-37`), and the uniform `priorityClass:2, wakeType:0, SubscribeTimer(i,1)` registration constants. The configuration decision domain has no owner (feeds the A0 matrix draft).
- **N-21. Bus backpressure/fault semantics are undefined where it matters** (V3): managed deferred delivery **does not catch subscriber exceptions** while sync delivery does (`DomainEventBus.cs:152-187`) — a throwing deferred handler propagates out of `FlushDeferred` into the scheduler; managed queues are unbounded; the native Background 10 MB drop-oldest cap is enforced **only** inside `df_background_queue_force_coalesce` (`background_queue.cpp:312-332`), not on the publish or idle-slot-dispatch paths; `BACKPRESSURE`/`EXPAND` strategies are accepted as parameters but **not implemented** (`:124-130`).
- **N-22. Generation validation is enforced natively but collapsed by managed callers** (C10-supplement, V3): `world.cpp` gates every accessor on `is_alive` and bumps versions on destroy — the ABA protection is real and fails closed; but since managed systems only ever present version-0 ids, the check degenerates to "index never recycled". Plus the `IsValid` semantic drift (`EntityId.cs:38` vs `world.cpp:74-78`).
- **N-23. Determinism reality vs promises**: one ad-hoc `new Random(42)` in `MovementSystem.cs:35` is the entire RNG story (no service, no world seed); `Parallel.ForEach` makes within-phase order nondeterministic; VULKAN requires save-reproducibility while GPU is non-bit-exact and the design mitigation (CPU-canonical state at save) is explicitly "not implemented". No determinism class is defined anywhere (resolved by the TIME draft's D0/D1/D2).
- **N-24. FIELDS↔VULKAN secondary drifts** beyond C6: "field dispatches are non-blocking" (FIELDS §See also) vs К-L7 sync path "consumer call returns after fence signals" (VULKAN §2.3.1); FIELDS keyed to superseded G0–G7 numbering and a stale "v2.0 LOCKED" pin; VULKAN §3.4 numeric-vs-string field-id sketch (N-in-C6 family, distinct instances).

### 6.4 Enforcement-surface gaps

- **N-25. There is no CI.** No `.github/workflows/`, no pipeline config of any kind, anywhere (V2). Consequences: analyzer enforcement exists only at local build time; `PERFORMANCE.md`'s CI-pinned budgets are unenforceable (C12); native build is manual (`build.md:35-36`), never invoked by any script/pipeline; the native binary is not committed and rides along via copy-if-exists (`DualFrontier.Core.Benchmarks.csproj:40-43`); Linux load requires a manual rename (`build.md:24`). The native CMake config has no warnings-as-errors and no sanitizers (`CMakeLists.txt:19-23`); tests are a custom `DF_CHECK` runner.
- **N-26. Analyzer coverage vs declaration**: 30 declared rule IDs in ANALYZER_RULES vs 17 implemented (13 deferred — honestly, with named gates: К-L20 cascade, hardware-tier cascade); no `[SystemAccess]`-completeness analyzer exists in any registry despite MIGRATION_PLAN's normative dependence (N-13). Reverse staleness: THREADING (06-12) and ISOLATION still describe rules as "non-detecting stubs" — A'.9.1 (07-02) shipped real detection; the *authoritative* docs now understate the *non-authoritative* reality.
- **N-27. InternalsVisibleTo load-bearing census** (V3): `Core→Systems` is effectively **unused** (only `nameof` on public interfaces + one doc comment) — removable now; `Core→Application` is heavily load-bearing (`GameServices` ×24, `SystemMetadata` ×14, `ParallelSystemScheduler` ×11, `DependencyGraph` ×8, `TickScheduler` ×7); `Core.Interop→Application` is load-bearing for exactly one type (`internal NativeMethods` used by `ManagedBusBridge`, `SchedulerAdapter`); `Application→Launcher` load-bearing (`GameLoop` ×9, `GameBootstrap` ×8). This turns the review's "inventory friend-access symbols" recommendation into a finished inventory with one immediately-actionable row.

## 7. Revised cross-cutting model set → authored drafts

The review proposed 2 new Tier-1 docs + 4 extensions of LOCKED docs + 1 skeleton. Extensions of LOCKED documents are out of session scope (read-only discipline), so extension-content is authored as standalone proposals with explicit fold-in plans. Session synthesis merged A1+A4-adjacent identity content and distributed A8, yielding **seven AUTHORED drafts**, all written this session under `docs/architecture/`, all carrying a mandatory preamble (authored-proposal / normative-target; NOT current truth; ratification per FRAMEWORK §7; conflicts resolve in favor of LOCKED docs until ratified; named fold-in targets):

| Draft | Covers | Fold-in on ratification |
|---|---|---|
| `EXECUTION_AUTHORITY_MATRIX.md` | A0 + cutover gates + deletion triggers + §14.7 precedence proposal + observability IDs | matrix → ARCHITECTURE.md; gates → KERNEL_ARCHITECTURE |
| `CONCURRENCY_AND_MEMORY_MODEL.md` | A1: thread census, owner-thread table, resource×operation matrix, happens-before catalog, lock order, shutdown semantics, fault-crossing symmetry | THREADING.md; Vulkan rows → VULKAN_SUBSTRATE |
| `RESOURCE_OWNERSHIP_AND_LIFETIME.md` | A2: ownership tree, per-resource lifetime table, lease/pinning law, shutdown law, deferred reclamation states | new LOCKED doc; per-resource rows → ECS/FIELDS/MOD_OS/VULKAN |
| `ENGINE_LIFECYCLE_AND_TRANSACTIONS.md` | A3 + A8(recovery): seven-stage vocabulary (prepare/validate/quiesce/commit/reclaim/recover/resume), transition inventory, extended mod state machine, fault taxonomy | MOD_OS §9 amendment (resolves C7); ISOLATION |
| `TIME_AND_CONSISTENCY_MODEL.md` | A4: canonical time vocabulary, the visibility table, pause/save boundaries, determinism classes D0/D1/D2, RNG law | THREADING.md; class assignments → KERNEL Part 0 / COMBO / VULKAN amendments |
| `IDENTITY_AND_ABI_CONTRACT.md` | A5+A6+A8(errors): identity registry table, version-0 resolution, C ABI protocol (versioning, ownership, validity windows, thread affinity), error taxonomy | KERNEL_ARCHITECTURE new part / Part 7 expansion; ECS/VULKAN example fixes |
| `PERSISTENCE_SNAPSHOT_CONTRACT.md` | A7 (skeleton — invariants only): canonical snapshot boundary, inclusion/exclusion table, identity serialization law, missing/updated-mod policy, schema/migration policy | standalone; constrains ECS/FIELDS/EVENT_BUS/MOD_OS choices before the save milestone |

Register enrollment: each as `DOC-A-*`, Category A, Tier 1, lifecycle **AUTHORED** with `special_case_rationale` (Tier-1+AUTHORED is a forbidden validator pair absent rationale; precedent: K_CLOSURE_REPORT, ANALYZER_RULES), version 0.1.0.

## 8. Recommendations vs the review's AD-1..AD-4

**AD-1 (truth normalization) — AGREE, amended.** The 12-item fix list shrinks to 11 (drop C9 — no contradiction to fix; keep the `Kernel.Abstractions` question as design work) and gains: KERNEL Part 1/4/8 rewrite-or-fence (N-2), C5 re-scoped to "retire or truth-sync the Draft TYPE_SYSTEM/TRACK_B pair" (N-13), reverse-staleness sweep of THREADING/ISOLATION analyzer statements (N-26), RESOURCE_MODELS banner (N-7), MODDING example fixes (N-10), capability-grammar unification (N-11), mod-fault timing unification (N-8, N-9). Role-marking (normative-current/target/evidence) is validated by this session's corpus map (§2) — and must include fixing N-1: the canonical К-L text must live in an authority-surface document (either promote a consolidated К-L section in KERNEL Part 0 to sole-canonical, or re-lifecycle K_CLOSURE's §2 as the exception with rationale).
**AD-2 (ownership/concurrency/lifecycle laws) — AGREE; delivered as drafts.** The seven drafts in §7 are the concrete starting texts; ratification order suggestion: EXECUTION_AUTHORITY_MATRIX first (it names owners the others cite), then LIFECYCLE+OWNERSHIP (they close the P0-class shutdown/unload gaps N-19), then TIME/IDENTITY-ABI, PERSISTENCE skeleton last-before-save-milestone.
**AD-3 (control-plane cutover) — AGREE, sharpened.** The missing artifacts are now named: no doc-level cutover gate exists (only `GameBootstrap.cs:157-159` comments), no deletion trigger for `DependencyGraph`, zero dashboard verification rows for scheduler cutover (vs 3 for bus). The authority-matrix draft §3 specifies gate conditions and deletion triggers; adopt them into the roadmap's Native foundation tracks. Equivalence tests must carry their own deletion gate (review's point, confirmed).
**AD-4 (boundary enforcement) — AMEND.** (a) `Components → Core.Interop` is not a doc contradiction (C9 refuted) — treat `Kernel.Abstractions` extraction as a design improvement with its own cost/benefit, not a truth fix; (b) IVT: `Core→Systems` is removable **now** at zero cost (N-27); the rest of the inventory is done — remaining work is classification, exactly as the review proposed; (c) ABI/error/identity contract: draft delivered (§7); (d) "update performance gates" understates reality — there is nothing to update: **create** the enforcement layer (N-25) or de-claw PERFORMANCE.md's enforcement claims.
**AD-5 (new; enforcement infrastructure reality).** Stand up minimal CI (managed build + tests + analyzers; native CMake build + selftest; later benchmarks), implement or delete `PerformanceGate`, add `-Werror`-equivalent and a sanitizer job to the native build, commit-or-document native binary provenance, and resolve the analyzer schism (N-13): either ratify ANALYZER_RULES as the family authority and retire the DF_TS drafts, or reconcile them — today both claim the same domain.

## 9. What NOT to change

Confirming and extending the review's §11 stability list, now with code-side proof:

- **The C++ kernel, the ECS/Fields split, the absence of a global generic event broker** — all validated; nothing found this session argues against them.
- **The Vulkan 1.3 hardware floor and fail-fast policy** — architecturally coherent and correctly enforced (`HardwareCapabilityCheck.cs:23`); only FIELDS' wording must follow (C6).
- **`CpuKernels/*` are not dead code and not a fallback to build** — they are the GPU equivalence oracle and the designed source of canonical field state for future saves (VULKAN §7.1/§7.2.1); keep them exactly as scoped.
- **The tests-only scheduler adapter and `BusFacade` are not cruft** — they are the cutover scaffolding К-L12/К-L15 require; keep until the cutover gates (authority-matrix draft §3) pass, then delete per their deletion triggers.
- **The Contracts-only mod compile surface is already real** (V1: every mod and fixture csproj) — do not weaken it while unifying the documentation story (C8).
- **`MOD_PIPELINE`'s Apply transaction is the model to generalize, not fix** — "the graph is always consistent — either the old working one, or the new fully built one" is the only fully-realized prepare/commit in the engine; the lifecycle draft's vocabulary §2 maps it verbatim.
- **The strict-version mod strategy** (caret+exact, no tilde, no backtracking) — consistent with the project axioms; unchallenged by any finding.
- **Documentation volume** — the corpus does not need shrinking for human brevity; it needs role marking (§2) and truth-scrub cadence (§5 systemic pattern). The code-truth rewrite discipline demonstrably works — THREADING and EVENT_BUS survived this audit almost untouched.
- **The governance register itself** — 291 documents, zero validation errors, working tooling; the defects found (N-3, N-4, N-5) are amendments to a live system, not grounds for replacement.

## 10. Synthesis

**Positioning against the review's 7.5/10.** The review's core thesis — *the macro-decomposition is strong; what is missing is decomposition by decision rights* — survives full verification: 10 of 12 contradictions confirmed against verbatim text and code anchors, all nine cross-cutting contract gaps confirmed or worse. Its two errors both matter in the same direction: C9 does not exist as stated (the umbrella is honest about `Components → Core.Interop`), and C5's stale side is a Draft outside the authority surface — meaning the *authoritative* corpus is somewhat more internally consistent than the review's framing implies, while the *authority mechanism itself* is weaker than the review knew (N-1: canonical К-L texts outside the surface; N-3: no precedence rule; N-13: two analyzer programs claiming the same domain).

**The session's own thesis, extending the review's.** Dual Frontier's decomposition problem is recursive: the same "one responsibility → one authority" defect the review found between the native and managed control planes exists **inside the documentation system** (canonical texts in non-authoritative docs; two normative layers in one LOCKED doc; two analyzer laws), **inside the enforcement layer** (budgets pinned to a nonexistent CI gate; declared-but-unimplemented rules; reverse staleness where authoritative docs understate shipped reality), and **at the process boundary** (shutdown — the one transition nobody owns, N-19). The corrective is also recursive: the same matrix/cutover/deletion-trigger discipline the review prescribed for schedulers applies verbatim to documents, analyzers and gates.

**Score movement.** Agreeing with ~7.5/10 overall, with two component adjustments: document-authority clarity is *lower* than the review's 6.0 (the N-1/N-3/N-13 findings are mechanism defects, not just mixed roles) — 5.5/10; runtime authority decomposition is marginally *better* than 5.5 given EVENT_BUS/THREADING's exemplary current-truth honesty and the verified-clean mod compile surface — 6.0/10. Neither adjustment changes the headline: after AD-1 + AD-2 (the seven drafts ratified, the 11-item truth normalization executed), 8.5/10 is honestly reachable before any native cutover; AD-3's gates make 9/10 falsifiable rather than aspirational; AD-5 is what makes any of the numbers *enforced* rather than declared.

**Deliverables of this session.** This report; the committed input review; seven AUTHORED draft models (§7) enrolled at Tier 1 with rationale; register bump 2.25 → 2.26. No existing document or source line was modified.

---

## Appendix A. Per-document notes (compressed)

One line of session-relevant essence per active doc beyond what §§2–6 already state.

- **ARCHITECTURE** — umbrella's real self-tension: intro "event routing are native authority" vs Layer map housing managed domain buses; mods-row misattributes reference enforcement to ALC.
- **KERNEL_ARCHITECTURE** — Part 0 solid; §1.10 type-id law and Part 7 error conventions are the best A5/A6 fragments in the corpus; header chronicle carries decisions found nowhere else (N-14).
- **KERNEL_FULL_NATIVE_SCHEDULER** — К-L12..К-L19 texts + 46 items + ~42 open Q-N; asserts "native scheduler live" while delegating forward state to ROADMAP; TLA+ pending; no executor-parallelism decision (Item 15).
- **THREADING** — the model citizen: states dual registration, empty sets, Timer-1 wakes, Planned switch; sole defect: pre-A'.9.1 analyzer status (reverse-stale) and the self-noted "same edges reused" inconsistency.
- **ECS** — clean surface; two teaching defects (version-0 loop; comment implying deleted runtime check); no semantics stated for wrong-version reads of live entities.
- **PERFORMANCE** — see C12; also budget attaches to the managed `DependencyGraph` facade while the authoritative native graph has no budget at all.
- **EVENT_BUS** — best current-truth doc; inverted observability (N-21 relative); [Immediate] unused in production; Magic resolution handlers are marked roadmap stubs.
- **CONTRACTS** — canonicity delegated to a mutable src file; evolution rules provably optimistic (new-bus bullet breaks implementers; record-field claim ignores binary compat); version gate one-directional.
- **FIELDS** — storage contract solid and shipped; everything GPU-side predates Q-G-2 (superseded G-ids, unbuilt DispatchCompute, "non-blocking" mischaracterization, stale VULKAN pin).
- **VULKAN_SUBSTRATE** — strongest A2/A4 fragment source (slot states, fences, waitIdle discipline); internal identity drift §3.4; no device-lost, no swapchain-recreation transaction protocol; input forwarding admitted absent.
- **MOD_OS_ARCHITECTURE** — richest lifecycle fragments (quiescence protocol, WeakReference pump, leaked-reference honesty); §9-vs-§9.5.1 is the A3 symptom; capability grammar self-contradiction (§3.2 vs §2.3).
- **MOD_PIPELINE** — the engine's only realized prepare/commit transaction; two-era body (4-member vs 15-member enum claims).
- **MODDING** — ALC refusal list is the concrete boundary law; quickstart example impossible under it (C8's sharpest instance).
- **ISOLATION** — honest deletion record + explicit enforcement-gap window; fault sequence conflicts with MOD_OS/MOD_PIPELINE timing (N-8).
- **OWNERSHIP_TRANSITION** — verified-shipped gameplay protocol; single-writer + startup write-conflict crash is a good pattern; DEBUG/RELEASE behavior fork noted (feeds A8 law).
- **COMBO_RESOLUTION / COMPOSITE_REQUESTS** — honest TARGET stubs; ordinal-collision and TransactionId-assignment under-specifications recorded for their Phase 4/5 milestones.
- **RESOURCE_MODELS** — needs the sibling honesty banner (N-7).
- **FEEDBACK_LOOPS** — previous-tick snapshot rule is the load-bearing A4 fragment; keep; resolve its DD-1 self-flag.
- **FHE_INTEGRATION_CONTRACT** — dormant-by-design with minimal current footprint (verified: two empty interfaces, zero consumers); activation gate externally contingent (industry event) — a permanent Tier-1 dormant occupant, fine once role-marked.
- **MIGRATION_PLAN** — its §5 delete/revert law is a genuine A3 fragment; internal arithmetic staleness; normative dependence on a nonexistent analyzer rule (N-13).
- **ARCHITECTURE_TYPE_SYSTEM / TRACK_B_ACTIVATION** — see N-13; both anchor on deleted symbols; both carry Draft-vs-"RATIFIED/LOCKED v0.1" self-contradictions in their own status lines.
- **ANALYZER_RULES** — verified 1:1 against disk; honest deferral tables; its own §4.2 admits the earlier "enforcement surface" overclaim era — the corpus's best example of a truth-scrubbed doc, sitting at the wrong authority standing.
- **FRAMEWORK** — operative/target split declared; predicate defects N-3/N-4/N-5; gate catalog + failability law are strong governance assets to keep.
- **PROJECT_AXIOMS** — PA-003's three-graph example stands as written (the review's proposed proof-obligation refinement remains a good amendment); PA-001 anchor broke (N-4).
- **SYNTHESIS_RATIONALE** — provenance clean; lockstep promise breached by FRAMEWORK 2.0 (N-4).
- **K_CLOSURE_REPORT** — N-1; also the only doc recording the bus adapter's orphaned→wired transition (Bug #2), i.e., real cutover evidence exists for the bus and not the scheduler.
- **A_PRIME_9_RECONNAISSANCE_REPORT** — pure evidence; treats native scheduler as live (inherits K_CLOSURE's overstatement).
- **PHASE_A_PRIME_SEQUENCING** — historical; does not contain F-34/Cascade B (those live in FRAMEWORK §14.9 + the REGISTER_INVERSION measure report).
- **K_L14_EVIDENCE_DASHBOARD** — 14 verifications; 3 bus rows, 0 scheduler-cutover rows (the C1 asymmetry metric).
- **K_EXTENSIONS_LEDGER** — narrative ledger; convention breaks N-12.

## Appendix B. Method artifacts

**Reader batches (P1).** R1 kernel-core: KERNEL_ARCHITECTURE, THREADING, ECS, PERFORMANCE. R2 native-scheduler: KERNEL_FULL_NATIVE_SCHEDULER, OWNERSHIP_TRANSITION. R3 gpu: VULKAN_SUBSTRATE, FIELDS. R4 mod-platform: MOD_OS, MOD_PIPELINE, MODDING, ISOLATION. R5 meta: FRAMEWORK, PROJECT_AXIOMS, ARCHITECTURE, CONTRACTS, SYNTHESIS_RATIONALE. R6 target-state: MIGRATION_PLAN, TRACK_B, TYPE_SYSTEM, ANALYZER_RULES. R7 events/protocols: EVENT_BUS, COMBO, COMPOSITE, RESOURCE_MODELS, FEEDBACK_LOOPS, FHE, K_EXTENSIONS_LEDGER. R8 evidence-skim (grep-then-read): K_CLOSURE_REPORT, A_PRIME_9_RECON, PHASE_A_PRIME_SEQUENCING, K_L14_DASHBOARD.

**Extraction template fields.** DOC header (id/path/tier/lifecycle/AUTHORITATIVE-§14.7) · ROLE · AUTHORITY (fixed 12-domain vocabulary: scheduling, event-routing, entity-identity, storage/memory, mod-lifecycle, gpu, threading, error-handling, persistence, time/tick, boundary/layering, configuration) · CONTRACTS [A0..A8] · XREF (defers-to|overrides|duplicates|tension) · CODE-CLAIMS (≤8) · SELF-CONTRADICTIONS · ADMITTED-GAPS · REVIEW-CHECK (MATCHES-REVIEW|MISCHARACTERIZED|NOT-FOUND) · NOVEL (≤3).

**Verifier probes (P2).** V1: mod/fixture reference census (C8), AI subsystem governance (G2), Persistence reality (G3), fixture contract surface (G4), FHE/Crypto reality (G5). V2: PERFORMANCE symbol staleness (C12), A0–A8 keyword absence sweeps, analyzer declared-vs-implemented-vs-wired diff (G7), CI/perf-gate reality (G8), determinism/RNG census (G10), native build/test/CI (G11). V3: presentation/input bridge (G1), hardcoded-config census (G9), IVT consumption census (G12), bus overflow/reentrancy/fault semantics (G13), shutdown/quiesce ordering (G15), generation-validation supplement (C10).

**Verdict criteria.** A contradiction verdict required: verbatim quote of each side with §-anchor, plus a code anchor where the claim touches code; UNVERIFIED rows were re-checked by direct grep before finalization. Authority-weight computed from the §14.7 predicate using register lifecycle/tier as rendered in each doc's frontmatter mirror.

**Tooling.** pwsh 7.6.3 (apt, packages.microsoft.com), powershell-yaml 0.4.12, .NET SDK 10.0.302 (dotnet-install); GitHub egress blocked by session policy (403) — Microsoft feeds used instead. Baseline register validation: exit 0, 25 pre-existing orphan advisories, 291 documents (unchanged; out of scope).
